The Case for Production-Grade Tooling

By Dan Tomescu — March 14, 2026

Most demo projects collapse under real traffic because they skip the unglamorous parts: timeouts, retries, rate limiting, and input validation. A weekend demo fetches a URL with a bare client and prints whatever comes back.

What production actually requires

Production code assumes the network is hostile. Every outbound request is bounded by an explicit timeout, gated by a rate limiter, and retried with backoff when the upstream returns a transient error. Untrusted input is validated before it can reach a socket.

  • Explicit connect and read timeouts.
  • Exponential backoff with jitter on 429 and 5xx responses.
  • SSRF protection that resolves hostnames before connecting.

The difference between a demo and a service is almost entirely in this defensive plumbing, and it is exactly what a discerning client looks for.
